thought: I was just lucky. It wasn't actually my talent or hard work.

reading.title: Poetic Empathy Shield
reading.description: Read verses of shared struggle to realize you are not alone. What is one specific, hard-earned creative achievement you've worked for that you can celebrate in a line of poetry?
reading.placeholder: e.g., I spent three weeks refining my rhyme scheme and rhythm until the poem sang.

writing.title: Reframing Quill
writing.description: Write a brief couplet or stream-of-consciousness line acknowledging your growth. What is a skill you've built over the last year?
writing.placeholder: e.g., Though the path was steep, I learned to write, and stepped from shadow into light.

building.title: Structured Haiku Hammer
building.description: Channel your focus. Write a simple 3-line haiku (5-7-5 syllables) focusing on one tiny detail of your hard work.
building.placeholder:
e.g., Staring at the page (5)
Ink flows from my wooden pen (7)
Worth is built in words (5)

painting.title: Sensory Metaphor Lens
painting.description: Describe your hard work as a sensory image (a warm flame, deep roots, etc.) instead of just 'luck'.
painting.placeholder: e.g., My competence is not a roll of dice; it is a deep-rooted oak that withstood the winter ice.

thought: They are going to realize I have no idea what I'm doing and find out I'm a fraud.

reading.title: Poetic Empathy Shield
reading.description: Believing you are a fraud means believing you tricked smart people. Read a stanza of a mentor's poem or think of positive feedback. Express it in a line of verse.
reading.placeholder: e.g., A friend read my stanza and said the imagery made them feel deeply understood.

writing.title: Reframing Quill
writing.description: No writer or creator knows everything; every voice is figuring it out. Write a line accepting your learning journey as a beautiful process.
writing.placeholder: e.g., I am a book still being written, and it is okay to not know the ending yet.

building.title: Structured Haiku Hammer
building.description: Create a haiku about the process of learning and being imperfect.
building.placeholder:
e.g., I step on the path (5)
Learning with every new line (7)
No fraud, just a start (5)

painting.title: Sensory Metaphor Lens
painting.description: Compare your fear of being 'found out' to a natural metaphor (fog clearing, masks dissolving) to strip its fear.
painting.placeholder: e.g., The fraud is a shadow cast by a small candle; when the sun rises, the shadow disappears.

thought: I must make no mistakes. If I fail at anything, it proves I'm incompetent.

reading.title: Poetic Empathy Shield
reading.description: The greatest poets wrote drafts full of crossings-out. Read or write a line about how mistakes make your writing unique.
reading.placeholder: e.g., Every ink smudge and crossed-out line is a footprint of where my mind has traveled.

writing.title: Reframing Quill
writing.description: Dethrone perfectionism. Write a couplet giving yourself permission to write bad first drafts.
writing.placeholder: e.g., Let the ink run messy, let the draft be crude; in the raw and broken, beauty is renewed.

building.title: Structured Haiku Hammer
building.description: Write a haiku about the value of making mistakes.
building.placeholder:
e.g., Stains upon the page (5)
Errors are the seeds of art (7)
Growth in every crack (5)

painting.title: Sensory Metaphor Lens
painting.description: Use the metaphor of Kintsugi (repairing ceramics with gold) to describe a mistake in your creative journey.
painting.placeholder: e.g., My errors are the golden seams that join the shattered pieces of my dreams.

thought: I shouldn't ask for help. If I do, they will know I don't belong here.

reading.title: Poetic Empathy Shield
reading.description: Poetry is a conversation across generations. Think of a time you shared or received advice. Write a line about community.
reading.placeholder: e.g., When we share our verses, we build a bridge that carries us over the silent river of doubt.

writing.title: Reframing Quill
writing.description: Collaboration is how writers grow. Write a line accepting help as a sign of strength, not weakness.
writing.placeholder: e.g., Asking for a hand is not a sign of defeat; it is the moment our voices meet.

building.title: Structured Haiku Hammer
building.description: Write a haiku about sharing your voice or seeking guidance from others.
building.placeholder:
e.g., Voices in the room (5)
Weaving lines of shared strength now (7)
Stronger when we ask (5)

painting.title: Sensory Metaphor Lens
painting.description: Describe collaboration or asking for help as a beautiful natural phenomenon (echoes in a canyon, birds flying in formation).
painting.placeholder: e.g., Like birds in flight who share the headwind's strain, we lift each other through the fog and rain.
